questions.question: List the six categories of the STRIDE threat model and the security property each one violates.

Describe, in detail, the four-step process for conducting a threat modeling session for a new web application feature that involves a file upload functionality. Include examples of threats and mitigations specific to this feature.
expectedPoints: Step 1: Decompose - Identify asset (uploaded files, server resources), entry point (file upload API), trust boundary (from user to server).
Step 2: Identify - Use STRIDE: Tampering (upload malicious file), DoS (upload huge file), Elevation of Privilege (upload executable for RCE).
Step 3: Mitigate - File type validation, anti-virus scanning, size limits, storing files outside web root.
Step 4: Validate - Document threats and review with team, add mitigations to backlog.
